visual_base.visual_narrative_log: (1) The video opens with a graphic-heavy intro set against a backdrop of a large green stuffed animal in a retail warehouse. An animated character's head from a popular film appears on the left. Bright pink and red stylized text overlays dominate the upper and center regions of the frame, indicating a weekly special report. The lighting is bright and industrial, characteristic of a large warehouse store. (2) A series of rapid cuts begins, showcasing various household products on store shelves. First, purple packs of Kleenex tissues are shown, followed by refrigerated packages of thinly sliced beef. The camera uses a mix of static shots and slight pans to emphasize the products. Large yellow and pink text overlays provide pricing details. The background consists of typical metal warehouse shelving and refrigerated units. (3) The sequence continues with personal care items like adult diapers and snacks like spicy squid strips. Each product is centered in the frame, with the camera often performing a quick zoom or tilt to draw attention to the packaging details. The lighting remains consistent, highlighting the textures of the plastic packaging and the labels. (4) The focus shifts to international food items and pantry staples, including Spanish pork belly, triangle-shaped chips, and dried cranberries. The camera moves quickly between different sections of the store. Text overlays remain high-density, clearly stating the original price and the discounted price for each item. (5) Various packaged sweets and snacks are presented, such as mushroom-shaped chocolate cookies, Maltesers, and cream wafers. The camera captures the bulk packaging typical of a warehouse club. The environment is clean and organized, with rows of identical boxes creating a repetitive pattern in the background. (6) Household and kitchen items like garlic bread crackers and corn chips are shown. The camera maintains a fast pace, matching the energetic narration. Text overlays are consistently placed in the center of the screen to ensure readability against the busy background of product labels. (7) The final segment displays large bags of rice, multipacks of probiotic drinks, and miscellaneous items like Disney-themed rugs and high-end whiskey. The camera pans across long aisles, giving a sense of the scale of the store. The lighting is bright and even throughout, with no significant shadows. The video ends with a display of red wine bottles on wooden shelves.

visual_human_presence.countingEvidence: No real humans are present; only products and graphics.

visual_scene_and_style.backgroundDescribe: The background consists of various sections of a Costco warehouse, including high-piled shelves, refrigerated open-display cases, and pallets of bulk items. Industrial lighting and large aisle signs are visible.

audio_base.audio_narrative_log: (1) The audio begins with a high-energy, fast-paced male narrator introducing the Costco weekly special report. The narrator's pitch is energetic and varied, aimed at grabbing attention immediately. Accompanying the voice is a bright, upbeat electronic BGM with a steady 128 BPM 4/4 beat. (2) A distinctive digital 'pop' sound effect triggers as the narrator begins listing the first few products, Kleenex and beef. The vocal delivery is rapid, with almost no pauses between sentences. The background music maintains its steady rhythmic drive, providing a consistent energy floor. (3) Each transition between product categories is marked by a sharp, percussive SFX, similar to a bubble popping or a camera shutter. The narrator continues to shout out product names and prices with high emotional arousal. Ambient warehouse noise is virtually nonexistent, suggesting a professional studio voiceover track mixed with clear BGM. (4) As the video progresses through the snack and beverage sections, the narrator's tone remains consistently animated and positive. The BGM synthesizers pulse in the background, carefully leveled below the vocal track to ensure clarity. The sound effects density is high, with an effect occurring roughly every two seconds. (5) In the final half of the video, covering household goods and alcohol, the vocal rate remains at an extreme high, nearing five syllables per second. The BGM maintains its major-mode tonality, reinforcing a joyful shopping atmosphere. No audio distortion is present, indicating high-quality mastering. (6) The video concludes with the narrator emphasizing the final wine deals. The background music reaches its loop point or fades slightly as the narration finishes. The overall acoustic signature is bright and compressed, optimized for mobile device playback and social media consumption.

narrative_and_purpose.mainPurpose: The video is designed to inform Costco members in Taiwan about current weekly discounts and promotional offers. By using high-speed editing, energetic narration, and clear pricing overlays, it aims to create a sense of urgency and excitement around the sales. The comprehensive intent is to drive foot traffic to the store during the specified dates.

endorsement_analysis.endorsementCues: The video showcases specific products available at a retail store (Costco) with clear pricing and discount information, acting as a direct promotional tool for the retailer and the featured brands.

high_inference.freeDescribe: The video effectively utilizes 'short-form' social media aesthetics, characterized by high informational density and rapid sensory stimuli. The synchronization between visual cuts, pricing text, and rhythmic narration makes it highly suitable for quick consumption.
